description.content:
Creates a new contract from a SpotDraft template and returns the created contract.

Provide the template id, contract data, and counterparty details needed to render the agreement. Use this endpoint when you want SpotDraft to generate the contract from structured data.

Creates a new contract from a SpotDraft template and returns the created contract.

Provide contract data, counterparty details, and any required signatories for the workflow.


In signatories list, sign_order should be set for either all items or no items of the list. Additionally when
sign_order is set for all items of signatories list then at-least one counter party signatory user should also be part
of the list.

The `contract_data` object must use **template field names as keys**. Each value should match the data type configured for that field in SpotDraft.

| Field type | Expected value | Example |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------- |
| String / text | String | `"Acme Inc."` |
| Multi-line text | String with line breaks if needed | `"Not applicable\nCustomer redlines pending"` |
| Number | Integer or decimal | `15` or `3.14` |
| Boolean / checkbox | Boolean | `true` |
| Date | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `"2026-03-28"` |
| Datetime | ISO 8601 timestamp | `"2026-09-15T10:30:00Z"` |
| Dropdown | Selected option value | `"annual_invoice"` |
| Multi-select | Array of selected option values | `["US", "IN"]` |
| Currency | `{ "type": "<ISO currency>", "value": number }` | `{ "type": "USD", "value": 12000 }` |
| Duration | `{ "days": int, "type": "DAYS\|MONTHS\|YEARS", "value": int }` | `{ "days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6 }` |
| Address | Object with address fields | `{ "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue", "city": "New York", "country": "United States" }` |
| Repeating group | Array of row objects | `[{"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}}]` |
| File / attachment | File object or uploaded file reference supported by the workflow | `{ "file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf" }` |

Sample contract data:

```json
{
  "agreement_date": "2026-03-28",
  "renewal_review_at": "2026-09-15T10:30:00Z",
  "customer_name": "Acme Inc.",
  "billing_currency": {"type": "USD", "value": 12000},
  "subscription_term": {"type": "MONTHS", "value": 12, "days": 365},
  "auto_renewal": true,
  "notice_period_days": 30,
  "service_countries": ["US", "IN"],
  "billing_address": {
    "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue",
    "city": "New York",
    "state": "NY",
    "country": "United States",
    "zipcode": "10118"
  },
  "pricing_schedule": [
    {"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}},
    {"milestone": "Annual subscription", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 7000}}
  ],
  "msa_attachment": {"file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf"}
}
```

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

This API can be used to upload existing executed contracts to SpotDraft.
    `contract_name` is treated as a caller-provided display name and is not inferred from the file content.
    Counterparty names in `counterparty_details` are also caller-provided values used for display/association.

urlencoded.description: (Required) The display name of the executed contract that is visible to users. This is caller-provided metadata and is not inferred from file contents.

Generates a preview PDF using the supplied contract data without creating or updating a contract.

Use this to validate how template variables render before you create the actual contract.


The `contract_data` object must use **template field names as keys**. Each value should match the data type configured for that field in SpotDraft.

| Field type | Expected value | Example |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------- |
| String / text | String | `"Acme Inc."` |
| Multi-line text | String with line breaks if needed | `"Not applicable\nCustomer redlines pending"` |
| Number | Integer or decimal | `15` or `3.14` |
| Boolean / checkbox | Boolean | `true` |
| Date | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `"2026-03-28"` |
| Datetime | ISO 8601 timestamp | `"2026-09-15T10:30:00Z"` |
| Dropdown | Selected option value | `"annual_invoice"` |
| Multi-select | Array of selected option values | `["US", "IN"]` |
| Currency | `{ "type": "<ISO currency>", "value": number }` | `{ "type": "USD", "value": 12000 }` |
| Duration | `{ "days": int, "type": "DAYS\|MONTHS\|YEARS", "value": int }` | `{ "days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6 }` |
| Address | Object with address fields | `{ "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue", "city": "New York", "country": "United States" }` |
| Repeating group | Array of row objects | `[{"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}}]` |
| File / attachment | File object or uploaded file reference supported by the workflow | `{ "file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf" }` |

Sample contract data:

```json
{
  "agreement_date": "2026-03-28",
  "renewal_review_at": "2026-09-15T10:30:00Z",
  "customer_name": "Acme Inc.",
  "billing_currency": {"type": "USD", "value": 12000},
  "subscription_term": {"type": "MONTHS", "value": 12, "days": 365},
  "auto_renewal": true,
  "notice_period_days": 30,
  "service_countries": ["US", "IN"],
  "billing_address": {
    "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue",
    "city": "New York",
    "state": "NY",
    "country": "United States",
    "zipcode": "10118"
  },
  "pricing_schedule": [
    {"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}},
    {"milestone": "Annual subscription", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 7000}}
  ],
  "msa_attachment": {"file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf"}
}
```

This API endpoint supports uploading Third-Party Paper (TPP) contracts and optionally allows for a review request. `contract_type_id` must be linked to a published Third-Party Paper upload workflow. If this linkage is missing, the API can fail with `SD_CONTRACTS_00289`. It accepts intake form data. The Intake Form Data should have the following data types with the following output:
Type Data Format Sample
Duration {"days": int, "type": DurationKind, "value": int}
`DurationKind`: `DAYS` or `MONTHS` or `YEARS` 		{"days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6}
Date String formatted date in form YYYY-MM-dd "2019-11-29"
Dropdown Value as returned in options. Can be string or number. "1"
String A string value. "Something"
Currency {"type": CurrencyCode, "value": float}
Where currencyCode: "INR", "USD", "SGD", "EUR" 		{"type": "INR", "value": 15000.00}
Check-box A boolean value true
text-box A string value. Can be multi-line "Not Applicable\nBecause..."
number A numeric value. Can be int or float 4 or 3.14
### Sample Intake Form Data: ``` { "term": {"days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6}, "notes": "Not Applicable", "capacity": 15, "setup_fee": {"type": "INR", "value": 30000.00}, "start_date": "2019-11-29", "yes_discounts": false, "payment_method": "1", "suggestions": "No suggestions" } ```

Retrieve the text content of a contract in different formats.

        **Formats:**
        - `text`: Returns the full contract text as a single string
        - `segments`: Returns the contract text as an array of segments with metadata

        **Feature Flags:**
        This API requires `FULL_TEXT_SEARCH` to be enabled for the workspace.

        **Content Availability:**
        Content must be previously extracted to be available through this API.

        This API supports user scoping when the 'Enforce-User-Scoping'
    header is set to 'true'. When enabled, the API will only return content for
    contracts that the specified user has access to based on their permissions.

Updates the structured contract data used to populate the contract template.

Use this to refresh template field values before the contract is finalized. Historic contracts are not supported for this endpoint.


The `contract_data` object must use **template field names as keys**. Each value should match the data type configured for that field in SpotDraft.

| Field type | Expected value | Example |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------- |
| String / text | String | `"Acme Inc."` |
| Multi-line text | String with line breaks if needed | `"Not applicable\nCustomer redlines pending"` |
| Number | Integer or decimal | `15` or `3.14` |
| Boolean / checkbox | Boolean | `true` |
| Date | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `"2026-03-28"` |
| Datetime | ISO 8601 timestamp | `"2026-09-15T10:30:00Z"` |
| Dropdown | Selected option value | `"annual_invoice"` |
| Multi-select | Array of selected option values | `["US", "IN"]` |
| Currency | `{ "type": "<ISO currency>", "value": number }` | `{ "type": "USD", "value": 12000 }` |
| Duration | `{ "days": int, "type": "DAYS\|MONTHS\|YEARS", "value": int }` | `{ "days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6 }` |
| Address | Object with address fields | `{ "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue", "city": "New York", "country": "United States" }` |
| Repeating group | Array of row objects | `[{"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}}]` |
| File / attachment | File object or uploaded file reference supported by the workflow | `{ "file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf" }` |

Sample contract data:

```json
{
  "agreement_date": "2026-03-28",
  "renewal_review_at": "2026-09-15T10:30:00Z",
  "customer_name": "Acme Inc.",
  "billing_currency": {"type": "USD", "value": 12000},
  "subscription_term": {"type": "MONTHS", "value": 12, "days": 365},
  "auto_renewal": true,
  "notice_period_days": 30,
  "service_countries": ["US", "IN"],
  "billing_address": {
    "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue",
    "city": "New York",
    "state": "NY",
    "country": "United States",
    "zipcode": "10118"
  },
  "pricing_schedule": [
    {"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}},
    {"milestone": "Annual subscription", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 7000}}
  ],
  "msa_attachment": {"file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf"}
}
```

urlencoded.description: (Required) ID of the SpotDraft contract template used to render the preview.

(Required) A key-value object where each key must match a template field name from the contract template.

Values should use the data type expected by that template field. Common patterns include:
- `string`: free-form text
- `number`: integer or decimal values
- `boolean`: `true` or `false`
- `date`: `YYYY-MM-DD`
- `datetime`: ISO 8601 timestamp such as `2026-03-28T10:15:00Z`
- `duration`: `{ "type": "MONTHS" | "YEARS" | "DAYS", "value": number, "days": number }`
- `currency`: `{ "type": "USD" | "INR" | ... , "value": number }`
- `dropdown`: the selected option value as configured in the template
- `multi_select`: an array of selected option values
- `address`: an object such as `{ "line_one": ..., "city": ..., "country": ..., "zipcode": ... }`
- `repeating`: an array of row objects for repeating groups or dynamic tables
- `file`: a file object or uploaded file reference where the workflow supports attachments

Use template field names exactly as configured in SpotDraft.

(Required) A key-value object where each key must match a template field name from the contract template.

Values should use the data type expected by that template field. Common patterns include:
- `string`: free-form text
- `number`: integer or decimal values
- `boolean`: `true` or `false`
- `date`: `YYYY-MM-DD`
- `datetime`: ISO 8601 timestamp such as `2026-03-28T10:15:00Z`
- `duration`: `{ "type": "MONTHS" | "YEARS" | "DAYS", "value": number, "days": number }`
- `currency`: `{ "type": "USD" | "INR" | ... , "value": number }`
- `dropdown`: the selected option value as configured in the template
- `multi_select`: an array of selected option values
- `address`: an object such as `{ "line_one": ..., "city": ..., "country": ..., "zipcode": ... }`
- `repeating`: an array of row objects for repeating groups or dynamic tables
- `file`: a file object or uploaded file reference where the workflow supports attachments

Use template field names exactly as configured in SpotDraft.

Date String formatted date in form YYYY-MM-dd "2019-11-29"
Dropdown Value as returned in options. Can be string or number. "1"
String A string value. "Something"
Currency {"type": CurrencyCode, "value": float}
Where currencyCode: "INR", "USD", "SGD", "EUR" 		{"type": "INR", "value": 15000.00}
Check-box A boolean value true
text-box A string value. Can be multi-line "Not Applicable\nBecause..."
number A numeric value. Can be int or float 4 or 3.14
### Sample Intake Form Data: ``` { "term": {"days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6}, "notes": "Not Applicable", "capacity": 15, "setup_fee": {"type": "INR", "value": 30000.00}, "start_date": "2019-11-29", "yes_discounts": false, "payment_method": "1", "suggestions": "No suggestions" } ```

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

Updates the structured contract data used to populate the contract template.

Use this to refresh template field values before the contract is finalized. Historic contracts are not supported for this endpoint.


The `contract_data` object must use **template field names as keys**. Each value should match the data type configured for that field in SpotDraft.

| Field type | Expected value | Example |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------- |
| String / text | String | `"Acme Inc."` |
| Multi-line text | String with line breaks if needed | `"Not applicable\nCustomer redlines pending"` |
| Number | Integer or decimal | `15` or `3.14` |
| Boolean / checkbox | Boolean | `true` |
| Date | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `"2026-03-28"` |
| Datetime | ISO 8601 timestamp | `"2026-09-15T10:30:00Z"` |
| Dropdown | Selected option value | `"annual_invoice"` |
| Multi-select | Array of selected option values | `["US", "IN"]` |
| Currency | `{ "type": "<ISO currency>", "value": number }` | `{ "type": "USD", "value": 12000 }` |
| Duration | `{ "days": int, "type": "DAYS\|MONTHS\|YEARS", "value": int }` | `{ "days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6 }` |
| Address | Object with address fields | `{ "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue", "city": "New York", "country": "United States" }` |
| Repeating group | Array of row objects | `[{"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}}]` |
| File / attachment | File object or uploaded file reference supported by the workflow | `{ "file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf" }` |

Sample contract data:

```json
{
  "agreement_date": "2026-03-28",
  "renewal_review_at": "2026-09-15T10:30:00Z",
  "customer_name": "Acme Inc.",
  "billing_currency": {"type": "USD", "value": 12000},
  "subscription_term": {"type": "MONTHS", "value": 12, "days": 365},
  "auto_renewal": true,
  "notice_period_days": 30,
  "service_countries": ["US", "IN"],
  "billing_address": {
    "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue",
    "city": "New York",
    "state": "NY",
    "country": "United States",
    "zipcode": "10118"
  },
  "pricing_schedule": [
    {"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}},
    {"milestone": "Annual subscription", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 7000}}
  ],
  "msa_attachment": {"file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf"}
}
```

description.content:
Creates a new contract from a SpotDraft template and returns the created contract.

Provide contract data, counterparty details, and any required signatories for the workflow.


In signatories list, sign_order should be set for either all items or no items of the list. Additionally when
sign_order is set for all items of signatories list then at-least one counter party signatory user should also be part
of the list.

The `contract_data` object must use **template field names as keys**. Each value should match the data type configured for that field in SpotDraft.

| Field type | Expected value | Example |
| ---------- | -------------- | ------- |
| String / text | String | `"Acme Inc."` |
| Multi-line text | String with line breaks if needed | `"Not applicable\nCustomer redlines pending"` |
| Number | Integer or decimal | `15` or `3.14` |
| Boolean / checkbox | Boolean | `true` |
| Date | `YYYY-MM-DD` | `"2026-03-28"` |
| Datetime | ISO 8601 timestamp | `"2026-09-15T10:30:00Z"` |
| Dropdown | Selected option value | `"annual_invoice"` |
| Multi-select | Array of selected option values | `["US", "IN"]` |
| Currency | `{ "type": "<ISO currency>", "value": number }` | `{ "type": "USD", "value": 12000 }` |
| Duration | `{ "days": int, "type": "DAYS\|MONTHS\|YEARS", "value": int }` | `{ "days": 180, "type": "MONTHS", "value": 6 }` |
| Address | Object with address fields | `{ "line_one": "350 Fifth Avenue", "city": "New York", "country": "United States" }` |
| Repeating group | Array of row objects | `[{"milestone": "Implementation", "amount": {"type": "USD", "value": 5000}}]` |
| File / attachment | File object or uploaded file reference supported by the workflow | `{ "file_name": "security-schedule.pdf", "content_type": "application/pdf" }` |

Sample contract data:

```json
{
  "agreement_date": "2026-03-28",
  "renewal_review_at": "2026-09-15T10:30:00Z",
```

Recipient lookups and participant-facing contract resources across public API versions.

A recipient is a person who can access the contract and perform an action. A recipient can be one of these 3 types:
Type Action
APPROVER Approve the contract
SIGNATORY Sign the contract
RECEIVER Receive the contract
Further, every action has a status associated with it. It can be one of these 3 types:
Action Status Description
COMPLETED User has successfully performed the action
IN_PROGRESS User has been invited but is yet to perform the action
PENDING User is yet to be invited to perform the action
This API can be used to get list of all recipients for a contract.

variable.description: (Required) ID of the Contract. Should be of the form T-123 or H-123. T stands for Template contracts and H stands for Historical contracts.

query.description: Filter by priority (exact match). Equivalent to filter__priority[$eq]=value. Allowed values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.
Filter by priority using $eq operator. Expects a single value for equality (e.g., string). Allowed values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.
Filter by priority using $in operator. Expects a comma-separated list of values (e.g., val1,val2). Use \, to include literal commas in values. Allowed values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.
Filter by priority using $ne operator. Expects a single value for not-equal comparison (e.g., string). Allowed values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.
Filter by status (exact match). Equivalent to filter__status[$eq]=value. Allowed values: DRAFT, PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, COMPLETED, DISCARDED.
Filter by status using $eq operator. Expects a single value for equality (e.g., string). Allowed values: DRAFT, PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, COMPLETED, DISCARDED.

Filter by status using $in operator. Expects a comma-separated list of values (e.g., val1,val2). Use \, to include literal commas in values. Allowed values: DRAFT, PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, COMPLETED, DISCARDED.
Filter by status using $ne operator. Expects a single value for not-equal comparison (e.g., string). Allowed values: DRAFT, PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED, COMPLETED, DISCARDED.

Create webhook subscriptions, inspect sample payloads, validate deliveries, and manage webhook secrets across public API versions.

Creates a webhook subscription for the selected SpotDraft activity types.
        See [Webhooks](/api/docs#description/webhooks) for delivery behavior, verification, and retry guidance.

urlencoded.description: (Required) The title of this webhook
(Required) Webhook activity types for which to trigger this webhook. The list cannot be empty.

(Required) The HTTPS URL that should receive this event. Non-HTTPS URLs are rejected.
The description for the webhook

Custom headers to be included in the webhook call. Header names are trimmed before validation. Use custom header names only; standard HTTP headers are rejected. Header names should start with `X-` and must not start with X-Google-, X-Goog-, X-GFE-, X-Amz-, X-MS-, X-DD-, X-Datadog-, X-B3-, X-SD- and X-SPOTDRAFT-.

Read-only analytics SQL execution and related analytics catalog guidance.

Runs **read-only** analytics SQL in **GoogleSQL** (Google Standard SQL), including for timestamps, dates, and functions. Submit exactly one **`SELECT`** statement (optional **`WITH`** / **`UNION`**). Inserts, updates, deletes, DDL, and multi-statement batches are rejected.

**Enablement**  
This capability must be enabled for your workspace. If it is not, the API returns **501** with code `SD_PUBLIC_00045`. Contact SpotDraft support to request access.

**Authentication**  
OAuth 2.0 Bearer JWT in the `Authorization` header (same Public API OAuth flow as other v2.1 endpoints). The authenticated principal must be authorized in the same organizational context as the token.

**Permission**  
**Insights view** (`INSIGHTS_VIEW`).

**Contracts scoping**  
By default, the **`Contracts`** table is limited to rows the authenticated user may access. Optional body field **`user_scoped`** or header **`X-Spotdraft-Analytics-User-Scoped`** (`true` / `false`, or `1` / `0`, `yes` / `no`) overrides that. When both are omitted, the default is **`true`** in this environment. **`user_scoped: false`** (or the header equivalent) allows workspace-wide contract rows only for users with the workspace **Admin** role; others receive **403** with `SD_PUBLIC_00044`.

**Tables and columns**  
Use **bare catalog table names** (e.g. `Contracts`, `Users`) and **snake_case** public column names from the table catalog (e.g. `execution_time`, `workflow_id`). Only allowlisted tables and columns are accepted.

**Allowlisted tables (quick reference)**  

| Table in SQL | Role |
|---|---|
| `Contracts` | Core contract fact table |
| `Users` | Users / org users |
| `Workflows_setup` | Workflow definitions (incl. frozen versions) |
| `Contract_types_setup` | Contract type lookup |
| `Counterparties_setup` | Counterparty directory |
| `Approvals_Setup` | Approval steps in workflows |
| `Entities_setup` | Legal entities |
| `Signatures_setups` | Signatory configuration per workflow |

For full column lists, types, and join guidance, see **[SpotDraft public analytics table catalog](#analytics-query-table-catalog)** (also under **External documentation**).

**Limits (this deployment)**  
- At most **5,000** rows per response; `truncated` is **true** when more rows would have matched.  
- Query job timeout: **60 seconds**.  
- Maximum bytes billed per query job: **500 MB** (500,000,000 bytes).  
- SQL text length limit: **10,000** characters per request.  

**Errors**  

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|---|---:|---|
| `SD_PUBLIC_00040` | 400 | Not an allowed read-only SELECT, or more than one statement |
| `SD_PUBLIC_00041` | 400 | References a table outside the allowlisted catalog |
| `SD_PUBLIC_00042` | 400 | Query could not be parsed or transpiled safely |
| `SD_PUBLIC_00043` | 502 | Query execution failed |
| `SD_PUBLIC_00044` | 403 | Workspace-wide contracts requested without workspace Admin role |
| `SD_PUBLIC_00045` | 501 | Analytics query API not enabled for this workspace |

Create and manage workspace tags used to categorize and filter contracts.

Bulk-create one or more workspace tags.

Tags are used to categorize, identify, and filter contracts within a workspace.
Use this endpoint to create multiple tags in a single request before assigning them to contracts.

**Required fields per tag:** `name`

**Optional fields per tag:**
- `colour_code` — hex colour string (e.g. `"#FF5733"`); defaults to empty string
- `tag_entity_type` — `"Contract"` (default) or `"Clause"`

**Responses:**
- `201 Created` — all tags were created successfully; returns the created tag objects with their IDs
- `400 Bad Request` — one or more tags already exist in the workspace, or the request body is invalid

# SpotDraft Public API

Welcome to the **SpotDraft Public API**. Use this documentation to authenticate requests, choose the right product workflow, and move from guide content into the endpoint-level API reference.

## Start here

If you are new to the API, use this sequence:

1. Confirm your **regional base URL / cluster** and authentication headers.

4. Verify redirects, authentication, errors, and webhook validation before going live.

## Versioning and conventions

- SpotDraft uses **versioning in the path**.
- Versioning is **scoped to each API family**, not to the full public API surface.
- Different families currently expose different path versions, including `v1`, `v2`, and `v2.1`.
- The version in the request path is the contract for that endpoint. Do not infer version availability across unrelated API families.
- A family can expose only one documented version, even if other families expose newer-looking path versions.
- Documentation links, tags, and operation references should match the exact versioned path shown in the schema for that endpoint.
- Composite ids such as `T-123` or `H-123` are identifiers, not plain integers. Send them exactly as documented.
- Datetimes are returned in ISO 8601 UTC form such as `2025-06-07T00:00:00Z`.
- Optional fields may be omitted or returned as `null`. Rely on field names and descriptions instead of assuming presence.
- Pagination uses `page` and `limit`. Version-specific list endpoints document any additional filters or sorting options.

## Base URL and regions

Pick the cluster that matches where your SpotDraft workspace is provisioned. The API reference server picker exposes the same four options.

| Region              | Domain                              |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| IN (India)          | `https://api.in.spotdraft.com/api/` |
| US (United States)  | `https://api.us.spotdraft.com/api/` |
| ME (Middle East)    | `https://api.me.spotdraft.com/api/` |
| EU (European Union) | `https://api.eu.spotdraft.com/api/` |

Always use **`https://`**. Keep your API calls, webhook configuration, and embedded flows in the same regional cluster where the workspace is provisioned.

## Redirects

The API commonly returns **`302 Found`** in two cases:

1. **HTTP instead of HTTPS**
2. **Trailing slash mismatch**

Some HTTP clients do not preserve the original method on redirect. Disable automatic redirects while debugging so you can fix the real URL issue directly.

# Authentication

All API requests must include a **Client ID** in the HTTP headers.

## Authentication quick reference

| Scenario | Required headers |
| -------- | ---------------- |
| Server-to-server request | `client-id`, `client-secret` |
| Act on behalf of a workspace user | `client-id`, `client-secret`, `user-email` |
| OAuth JWT request | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` |
| OAuth JWT request to a dual CLM bearer/OAuth endpoint | `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, `X-SD-Auth-Type: oauth-jwt` |
| Debugging auth failures | Confirm the workspace region, credential pair, and whether the impersonated user is active |

## Requests as the application

For server-to-server access, send both **Client ID** and **Client Secret** in headers. SpotDraft audits these calls as the integration, not as a browser user session.

### User impersonation

You may pass **`user-email`** with the email of a user to act on their behalf. That user must be an active member of the workspace tied to the credentials.

Typical uses:

- respecting per-user permissions
- user-scoped list endpoints
- audit attribution

If you omit **`user-email`**, the integration runs as the user linked to the API credentials.

Use impersonation only when you want permission checks and audit attribution to resolve to a specific workspace user. Keep the credential pair and the selected user in the same workspace and region.

## Header reference

| Type | Header | Value |
| ---- | ------ | ----- |
| HEADER | `client-id` | string |
| HEADER | `client-secret` | string |
| HEADER | `user-email` | string (optional) |
| HEADER | `Authorization` | `Bearer <token>` for OAuth JWT access |
| HEADER | `X-SD-Auth-Type` | `oauth-jwt` when an endpoint explicitly supports both standard CLM bearer tokens and OAuth JWT bearer tokens |

## OAuth JWT access

OAuth-enabled routes accept a user-scoped OAuth JWT in the standard `Authorization` header:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

Some internal routes support both standard CLM bearer tokens and OAuth JWT bearer tokens. For those dual-auth routes, callers must opt into OAuth JWT authentication explicitly:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <token>
X-SD-Auth-Type: oauth-jwt
```

This opt-in header prevents Django from treating an OAuth JWT string as a CLM bearer token before OAuth JWT authentication runs. Routes that only declare OAuth JWT authentication continue to use the standard bearer header without the opt-in header. Guide and guideline CRUD endpoints use the dual-auth pattern so existing CLM bearer-token clients continue to work while Sidebar and agent clients can call them with user-scoped OAuth JWTs.

## Interactive auth in the API reference

The API reference exposes the supported auth mechanisms per endpoint:

- `client-id` + `client-secret` for public API integrations
- `Bearer <token>` for OAuth-enabled routes
- `Bearer <token>` plus `X-SD-Auth-Type: oauth-jwt` for routes that explicitly support both standard CLM bearer tokens and OAuth JWT bearer tokens
- clickthrough-specific headers on clickthrough browser endpoints

`user-email` remains an optional request header for impersonation when the endpoint supports client-header authentication.

## Creating credentials

In SpotDraft, open **Settings → Developer settings**, then **Generate API credential**. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** at creation time and store them in a secrets manager or password vault.

If requests fail unexpectedly, first confirm:

- you are calling the correct regional cluster
- the credential pair belongs to the same workspace
- any impersonated `user-email` is active in that workspace

Reference: [Developer settings](https://help.spotdraft.com/articles/8632014145-developer-settings)

# Webhooks

Webhooks notify **your server** when contract lifecycle events happen in SpotDraft. Register one or more **HTTPS endpoints** in **Settings → Developer settings → Webhooks**, choose the event types, and SpotDraft delivers an HTTP `POST` with a JSON payload.

Return a **`2xx`** response quickly. Queue slow work so timeouts do not count as failed deliveries.

## Delivery requirements

- **HTTPS** with a publicly trusted certificate
- reliable endpoint behavior
- support for multiple webhook URLs per account
- event-type filtering so you only receive what you need
- fast acknowledgement with asynchronous downstream processing

## Verifying webhooks

Use **`X-SD-WEBHOOK-CONTENT-HASH`** for verification. Validate the raw request body with **HMAC-SHA512** using the `hmac_key` returned by the HMAC key API.

```python
import base64
import hashlib
import hmac

signature = hmac.new(
    base64.b64decode(sample_hmac_key),
    request.body,
    digestmod=hashlib.sha512,
).hexdigest()

assert signature == request.headers["X-SD-WEBHOOK-CONTENT-HASH"]
```

Compare against the **raw** request body bytes before any JSON parsing or normalization.

## Logs and troubleshooting

Developer Settings also exposes webhook logs for operational debugging. Use them to:

- search deliveries by contract id
- inspect the request payload sent by SpotDraft
- confirm delivery failures before retrying downstream processing

When investigating failures, confirm that the destination URL belongs to the same cluster strategy you use for the rest of the integration and that your endpoint returns a `2xx` quickly.

## Common activity types

| `activity` value | When it fires |
| ---------------- | ------------- |
| `CONTRACT_CREATED` | A new contract is created |
| `CONTRACT_SENT_TO_COUNTERPARTY` | Sent for counterparty review / redlining |
| `CONTRACT_SIGNATURE_REQUESTED` | Marked for signature |
| `CONTRACT_SIGNED` | Any required signatory completes signing |
| `CONTRACT_EXECUTED` | All signatures are complete |
| `CONTRACT_PROCESS_METRIC_UPDATED` | Workflow metrics change |

Use the API reference for version-specific webhook endpoints and sample payload operations.

Reference: [Developer settings](https://help.spotdraft.com/articles/8632014145-developer-settings)

# Errors

SpotDraft uses conventional HTTP status codes so clients can branch without parsing proprietary error enums first.

## Error handling guidance

- Treat the HTTP status code as the primary branch condition.
- Parse the response body for fields such as `detail`, `message`, `code`, or field-level validation errors.
- Preserve the original request URL and method in logs. Redirects and trailing-slash mismatches can otherwise look like unrelated auth or validation problems.

## HTTP status summary

| Code | Meaning |
| ---- | ------- |
| `200` | Success |
| `400` | Invalid or incomplete input |
| `401` | Credentials missing, expired, or not accepted |
| `402` | Request failed due to business-rule or workflow state |
| `403` | Authenticated, but not allowed |
| `404` | Resource not found or not visible to the caller |
| `409` | Conflict, duplicate, or version mismatch |
| `429` | Rate limited |
| `500`, `502`, `503`, `504` | SpotDraft or upstream infrastructure error |

## Retry guidance

- Retry `429` and `5xx` responses with exponential backoff.
- Do **not** blindly retry `4xx` responses without fixing the request.
- Disable automatic redirect following while debugging `302` responses so URL issues are visible.

# Embedded Contract API

Use the Embedded Contract flow to create contracts through the Public API and render SpotDraft’s signing experience inside your product with an iframe.

## High-level flow

1. Create the contract with user-supplied data.
2. Generate a short-lived embedded URL for the counterparty.
3. Render the iframe.
4. Listen for `postMessage` events to detect completion, decline, or error states.

## Step 1 — Create a contract

Authenticate with your application credentials and call the **Create Contract** API using the data you collect in your product.

Use a credential pair linked to the SpotDraft user who should:

- receive notifications
- appear as the business user when relevant
- own the integration-level audit trail

## Step 2 — Generate the embed URL

Use the contract id returned by Create Contract together with the counterparty email to call the embedded URL endpoint. Treat the returned URL like a capability token: load it only for the intended user session and avoid logging it without redaction.

## Step 3 — Render the iframe

Listen for `postMessage` events from the iframe and validate `event.origin` before trusting them.

| Event | Message name | Payload |
| ----- | ------------ | ------- |
| Success | `spotdraft.embedded.sign_successful` | `{ contract_id }` |
| Declined | `spotdraft.embedded.sign_declined` | `{ contract_id }` |
| Signing error | `spotdraft.embedded.sign_error` | `{ contract_id, error: { code, upstream_error } }` |

## Integration notes

- Large agreements can take several seconds to render. Show a loader or skeleton state.
- Keep API calls and embed URLs in the same region as the tenant.
- Handle iframe close / completion without polling where possible.

# Clickthrough SDK

The **Clickthrough** JavaScript SDK lets you capture recorded acceptance of one or more agreements before a user continues with signup, checkout, or access to a gated feature.

## Prerequisites

Before integrating, confirm the following in the SpotDraft Clickthrough console:

- Clickthrough is enabled for the workspace
- the relevant agreements are attached and published
- your supported domains are allowlisted
- production pages are served over HTTPS
- you are using the snippet values for the same regional cluster as your workspace

## Installation

### NPM

```bash
npm install @spotdraft/clickwrap-client
```

### CDN

```html
<script
  type="module"
  src="https://sdk.spotdraft.com/clickwrap/v1/sdk.js"
></script>
```

## Initialization

```typescript
import { SdClickthrough } from "@spotdraft/clickwrap-client";

const clickthrough = new SdClickthrough({
  clickwrapId: "CLICKWRAP_ID_FROM_CONSOLE",
  hostLocationDomId: "HOST_ELEMENT_DOM_ID",
  baseUrl: "BASE_URL_FROM_CONSOLE",
});

await clickthrough.init();
```

Use the `clickwrapId` and `baseUrl` exactly as provided by the SpotDraft console snippet for that clickthrough.
For the latest SDK package details, installation metadata, and published package versions, use the npm package page for `@spotdraft/clickwrap-client`.

## Submit payload

`user_identifier` is the only required payload field for `submit(payload)`. It should uniquely identify the user in your product, such as their email address or mobile number.

```typescript
const payload = {
  user_identifier: "johndoe@example.com",
  first_name: "John",
  last_name: "Doe",
  user_email: "johndoe@example.com",
};
```

## Common methods

| Method | Description |
| ------ | ----------- |
| `init()` | Mount embedded UI |
| `isAccepted()` | Check whether required agreements are accepted |
| `submit(payload)` | Record acceptance |
| `openConsentDialog()` | Open modal mode |
| `closeConsentDialog()` | Close modal mode |
| `isReacceptanceRequired(id)` | Check whether a user must accept again |

## Common events

| Event | When it fires |
| ----- | ------------- |
| `acceptanceToggled` | Aggregate acceptance changed |
| `acceptanceComplete` | All mandatory agreements are accepted |
| `cancelClicked` | User dismisses the flow |
| `sdClickthroughLoaded` | CDN SDK is ready |
| `sdClickthroughLoadFailed` | CDN SDK failed to load |

## Common setup issue

If the SDK returns **`Request received from invalid domain`**, the request origin is not allowlisted in Clickthrough settings. Add the full domain, including protocol such as `https://app.example.com`. `localhost` is typically allowlisted for local testing.

Use the API reference for clickthrough endpoints and keep the product console’s **View snippet** output as the source of truth for tenant-specific ids and base URLs.

References:

- [@spotdraft/clickwrap-client on npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@spotdraft/clickwrap-client)
- [How to Implement Clickthrough in Your Application](https://help.spotdraft.com/articles/3574375021-how-to-implement-clickthrough-in-your-application)
- [How to Implement SpotDraft Clickthrough in Mobile Application](https://help.spotdraft.com/articles/9120222930-how-to-implement-spotdraft-clickthrough-in-mobile-application)
